quiz_questions.question_text: What is the range of {2, 5, 8, 11, 20}?
quiz_questions.explanation: The range is the difference between the maximum and minimum values: 20 - 2 = 18.

quiz_questions.question_text: Find the range of {14, 22, 7, 35, 18}.
quiz_questions.explanation: Range = maximum - minimum = 35 - 7 = 28.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the interquartile range (IQR) of {1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13}?
quiz_questions.explanation: Q1 is the median of {1, 3, 5} = 3 and Q3 is the median of {9, 11, 13} = 11. IQR = Q3 - Q1 = 11 - 3 = 8.

quiz_questions.question_text: For the data set {4, 4, 4, 4, 4}, what is the range?
quiz_questions.explanation: Since all values are the same, the range is 4 - 4 = 0, meaning there is no spread.

quiz_questions.question_text: Find Q1 for the data set {2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16}.
quiz_questions.explanation: The lower half is {2, 4, 6, 8}. Q1 is the median of the lower half: (4 + 6) / 2 = 5.

quiz_questions.question_text: The five-number summary of a data set is: Min = 10, Q1 = 15, Median = 20, Q3 = 28, Max = 35. What is the IQR?
Which data set has a greater range: Set A = {3, 7, 10} or Set B = {1, 5, 20}?

options.text: Set A
They are the same
Set B
Cannot be determined

quiz_questions.explanation: Range of A = 10 - 3 = 7 and Range of B = 20 - 1 = 19. Set B has the greater range.

quiz_questions.question_text: Calculate the mean absolute deviation (MAD) of {2, 4, 6, 8, 10}.
Find Q3 for the ordered data set {5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35}.
quiz_questions.explanation: The upper half (values above the median of 20) is {25, 30, 35}. Q3 is the median of the upper half, which is 30.

quiz_questions.question_text: A data set has Q1 = 12 and Q3 = 26. What values would be considered outliers using the 1.5 x IQR rule on the upper end?

options.text: Values greater than 47
Values greater than 40
Values greater than 26
Values greater than 33

quiz_questions.explanation: IQR = 26 - 12 = 14. Upper fence = Q3 + 1.5 x IQR = 26 + 1.5(14) = 26 + 21 = 47. Any value greater than 47 is an outlier.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the range of a data set?

options.text: The difference between the maximum and minimum values
The average of all the values
The middle value in the data set
The sum of all values

quiz_questions.explanation: The range is the simplest measure of spread, calculated by subtracting the minimum value from the maximum value in the data set.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'interquartile range' (IQR) mean?

options.text: The range of the entire data set
The difference between the third quartile and the first quartile
The average of Q1 and Q3
The median of the data set

quiz_questions.explanation: The interquartile range (IQR) is the difference Q3 - Q1, representing the spread of the middle 50% of the data.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a 'quartile'?

options.text: A value that divides a data set into two equal parts
A value that divides a data set into four equal parts
A value that divides a data set into ten equal parts
The average of all values

quiz_questions.explanation: Quartiles divide an ordered data set into four equal parts. Q1 is the 25th percentile, Q2 (the median) is the 50th, and Q3 is the 75th.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'mean absolute deviation' (MAD) measure?

options.text: The average distance each data value is from the mean
The maximum distance from the mean
The difference between Q1 and Q3
The square of the average distance from the mean

quiz_questions.explanation: Mean absolute deviation (MAD) measures the average of the absolute values of each data point's deviation from the mean, indicating how spread out the data is.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a 'five-number summary'?

options.text: Mean, median, mode, range, and IQR
Minimum, Q1, median, Q3, and maximum
The five largest values in a data set
Five measures of center

quiz_questions.explanation: The five-number summary consists of the minimum, first quartile (Q1), median (Q2), third quartile (Q3), and maximum, providing a concise overview of the data's distribution.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'standard deviation' describe?

options.text: The middle value of the data
How far, on average, each data value is from the mean
The difference between the largest and smallest values
The most common value in the data set

quiz_questions.explanation: Standard deviation measures the typical distance of data values from the mean. A larger standard deviation indicates greater spread in the data.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'variance' mean in statistics?

options.text: The average of the data set
The square of the standard deviation
The range divided by 2
The median minus the mean

quiz_questions.explanation: Variance is the average of the squared deviations from the mean. It equals the standard deviation squared and measures how spread out the data values are.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'spread' refer to in a data set?

options.text: The typical value of the data
The shape of the distribution
How much the data values vary or are dispersed
The number of data values

quiz_questions.explanation: Spread (or variability) describes how much the data values differ from each other and from the center, measured by range, IQR, MAD, or standard deviation.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is an 'absolute deviation'?

options.text: The square of the difference between a data value and the mean
The positive distance between a data value and the mean
The difference between Q3 and Q1
The sum of all data values

quiz_questions.explanation: An absolute deviation is the absolute value of the difference between a data point and the mean, ensuring the distance is always positive.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does it mean when a data set has a 'small IQR'?

options.text: The data values are all the same
The middle 50% of the data values are close together
The range is also small
There are no outliers in the data

quiz_questions.explanation: A small IQR means the middle half of the data is tightly clustered, indicating low variability among the central data values.

quiz_questions.question_text: A gym tracks the number of visitors each day for a week: 45, 52, 48, 60, 55, 43, 50. The manager needs to report the spread to the owner. What is the range of daily visitors?
quiz_questions.explanation: Range = maximum - minimum = 60 - 43 = 17 visitors.

quiz_questions.question_text: A teacher recorded quiz scores for 9 students: 65, 70, 72, 75, 78, 80, 85, 88, 95. She wants to describe the spread of the middle half of scores. What is the IQR?
quiz_questions.explanation: Q1 is the median of {65, 70, 72, 75} = (70+72)/2 = 71. Q3 is the median of {80, 85, 88, 95} = (85+88)/2 = 86.5. IQR = 86.5 - 71 = 15.5, but using the standard method with the median included, Q1 = 71 and Q3 = 86.5 gives IQR = 15.5. Rounded: 15.

quiz_questions.question_text: Two pizza delivery services report their delivery times in minutes. Service A: {18, 20, 22, 19, 21} and Service B: {10, 15, 25, 30, 20}. Which service is more consistent, and how can you tell?

options.text: Service B, because its mean is higher
Service A, because its range is smaller
Service B, because its range is larger
They are equally consistent

quiz_questions.explanation: Range of A = 22 - 18 = 4 minutes. Range of B = 30 - 10 = 20 minutes. A smaller range means less variability, so Service A is more consistent.

quiz_questions.question_text: A plant nursery measured the heights (in cm) of 8 seedlings: 5, 7, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 25. Using the 1.5 x IQR rule, is the value 25 an outlier?

options.text: Yes, because 25 > Q3 + 1.5 x IQR
No, because 25 is within the fences
Yes, because 25 is the maximum
Cannot be determined

quiz_questions.explanation: Q1 = (7+8)/2 = 7.5, Q3 = (14+15)/2 = 14.5. IQR = 14.5 - 7.5 = 7. Upper fence = 14.5 + 1.5(7) = 14.5 + 10.5 = 25. Since 25 is equal to the fence, it is on the boundary; however, values at or above the fence are typically flagged as potential outliers.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student scored 70, 82, 78, 85, and 90 on five tests. Her teacher says the MAD for the class is 4.5 points. If the student's MAD is higher than the class, her scores are more spread out. What is the student's MAD?

A weather station recorded high temperatures (°F) for 10 days: 68, 72, 70, 75, 73, 71, 69, 74, 76, 72. The station in the next town recorded temperatures with a range of 15°F. Which station had more variable temperatures?

options.text: The first station
The second station
They have the same variability
Cannot be determined

quiz_questions.explanation: Range of the first station = 76 - 68 = 8°F. The second station's range is 15°F, which is larger, indicating more variable temperatures.

quiz_questions.question_text: A factory produces bolts that should be 10 cm long. A quality inspector measures 6 bolts: 9.8, 10.1, 10.0, 9.9, 10.2, 10.0. What is the range of the bolt lengths?

options.text: 0.3 cm
0.4 cm
0.2 cm
0.5 cm

quiz_questions.explanation: Range = maximum - minimum = 10.2 - 9.8 = 0.4 cm.

quiz_questions.question_text: A coach compares two runners' 100-meter dash times over 5 races. Runner A: {11.2, 11.5, 11.3, 11.4, 11.1} and Runner B: {10.5, 12.0, 11.0, 11.8, 11.2}. Which runner is more consistent?

options.text: Runner B, because their fastest time is better
Runner A, because the range of times is smaller
They are equally consistent
Runner B, because their mean is lower

quiz_questions.explanation: Range of A = 11.5 - 11.1 = 0.4 seconds. Range of B = 12.0 - 10.5 = 1.5 seconds. Runner A's smaller range indicates more consistent performance.

quiz_questions.question_text: A school cafeteria tracked the number of lunches sold each day for two weeks (10 days): 120, 135, 128, 145, 130, 125, 140, 132, 138, 127. What is the IQR of lunches sold?

A cell phone company compares battery life (in hours) for two models. Model X: {8, 9, 10, 9, 10, 8, 9} and Model Y: {6, 12, 7, 11, 8, 10, 9}. A customer wants the most reliable battery life. Which model should they choose, based on the MAD?

options.text: Model Y, because its mean is higher
Model X, because its MAD is smaller
Model Y, because its MAD is smaller
They have the same MAD

quiz_questions.explanation: Model X mean = 63/7 = 9. Deviations: 1,0,1,0,1,1,0. MAD = 4/7 ≈ 0.57. Model Y mean = 63/7 = 9. Deviations: 3,3,2,2,1,1,0. MAD = 12/7 ≈ 1.71. Model X has a smaller MAD, meaning more consistent battery life.
